channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Federal judge waits to decide about dismissing corruption charges against NYC Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: A federal judge did not make a determination on Wednesday whether to dismiss corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate GOP budget bill back on track hours after Trump threw it into turmoil
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Back on track, Senate Republicans pushed ahead Wednesday with their $340 billion budget bill focused on funding the White House's mass deportations and border security agenda after Vice President JD Vance gave a green light to proceed despite a morning dust-up caused by President D
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department official urges quick dismissal of criminal case against New York City mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — At an extraordinary hearing Wednesday, the Justice Department’s second-in-command demanded that a judge dismiss corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams, and the leader of the nation’s largest city — his liberty and political future at stake — declared: “I have not c
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, MICHAEL R. SISAK and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rep. Eli Crane stunned at 'insane' sum in taxpayer funds used for transgender animal studies
item.description: Congressional lawmakers were told earlier this month about the staggering price tag in taxpayer money used to conduct studies on animals.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: State Department makes 5 drug cartels terrorist organization
item.description: Tren de Aragua, MS-13 and the Sinaloa and Jalisco cartels are among those named terrorists by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Senators ask Trump's Labor Department nominee where her allegiance would lie
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Members of a Senate committee grilled Labor Secretary-designate Lori Chavez-DeRemer on Wednesday about her past support of pro-union legislation, her position on raising the federal minimum wage and her willingness to disagree with President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: CATHY BUSSEWITZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas Senate votes in favor of proposals to deny bail to certain accused violent criminals
item.description: The package was fast-tracked after Gov. Greg Abbott declared “bail reform” an emergency item. The bills now head to the House for consideration.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Pooja Salhotra, KPRC 2

item.title: Cuts to Veterans Affairs made in 'dishonest' way: Former employee
item.description: Employees fired included researchers looking into cancer treatments, opioid addiction and burn pit exposure.
item.dc:creator: Nichole Berlie, NewsNation

item.title: HUD offices became as vacant as a ‘Spirit Halloween’ store under Biden: admin sources
item.description: D.C.'s Housing and Urban Development headquarters is among the federal buildings that turned into empty "Spirit Halloween" stores under the Biden administration, Fox Digital learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minnesota business leader among thousands of federal workers axed in Trump cuts
item.description: Thousands of probationary employees who have not been in their jobs long enough to qualify for certain protections have been terminated by federal agencies around the country, including in Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: The Latest: Tensions rise between Trump and Zelenskyy over the Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: President Donald Trump on Wednesday called Volodymyr Zelenskyy a “dictator without elections,” lashing out after the Ukrainian president said Trump was being influenced by Russian disinformation as he moves to end the Kremlin’s war against Ukraine on terms that Kyiv says are too favorable to Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Should Hochul remove Adams? Former NY Gov. Paterson weighs in
item.description: Former New York Gov. David Paterson joins NewsNation's "CUOMO" to discuss the political turmoil surrounding NYC Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration halts support for representing unaccompanied children in immigration court
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration on Tuesday stopped support for legal representation in immigration court for children who enter the United States alone, a setback for those fighting deportation who can't afford a lawyer.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A federal judge has denied states‘ bid to halt DOGE and Musk‘s work
item.description: A federal judge has denied a bid to temporarily halt DOGE from its controversial work at certain federal agencies. Meanwhile, the White House says Elon Musk is not technically DOGE’s leader.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: DOGE is getting lists of the military's probationary workers from the Pentagon
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Department of Government Efficiency staffers were at the Pentagon on Tuesday and receiving lists of the military’s probationary employees, U.S. officials said.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Howard Lutnick as commerce secretary, a key role for Trump's trade agenda
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate confirmed wealthy financier Howard Lutnick as commerce secretary Tuesday, putting in place a staunch supporter of President Donald Trump's hardline trade polices.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says Zelensky 'should have never started' war with Russia
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday appeared to blame Ukraine's leaders for the three-year war with Russia, arguing Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky "should have never started it.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fentanyl on coroners' radar long before Nevada lawmakers took notice
item.description: The coroner's office knows what's happening in the community long before lawmakers do. That became clear during a discussion of deaths on Nevada's roads in Carson City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada may expand revenge porn law to cover AI
item.description: A proposal at the Nevada Legislature would amend the state’s revenge porn law to include computer-generated images.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New York's governor meets top political leaders as she weighs removing Mayor Eric Adams from office
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York Gov. Kathy Hochul held a series of meetings with key political figures Tuesday as she contemplates removing Mayor Eric Adams from his office, an unprecedented step that reflects the growing turmoil inside City Hall.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, JENNIFER PELTZ and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge refuses to block DOGE’s access to student borrower data
item.description: A judge declined to block Elon Musk’s DOGE from gaining access to Education Department data on student borrowers.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: 4 NYC deputy mayors resign after meeting with Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Four high-level officials in Mayor Eric Adams' administration resigned Monday after expressing concerns about the mayor's relationship with the Trump Administration, according to City Hall and sources familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, NewsNation

item.title: Social Security head steps down over DOGE data access: Sources
item.description: King's exit from the administration is one of several departures of high-ranking officials concerned about DOGE staffers' potential unlawful access to private taxpayer information.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans hope to jam through defense, border funding boost ahead of shutdown fight
item.description: Senate Republicans have the ambitious goal of jamming through funding boosts for defense and border security — separately from the appropriations process — ahead of a mid-March deadline to prevent a government shutdown. Senate Budget Committee Chair Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: European leaders scramble ahead of Trump’s Ukraine summit with Putin
item.description: European leaders were hopeful they could work with the Trump administration on the Ukraine war and other issues. But comments last week by several U.S. officials have poured cold water on such hopes.
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley, MPR News

item.title: Trump nominates Ed Martin, an ally who defended Jan. 6 rioters, to stay as top DC federal prosecutor
item.description: Conservative activist Ed Martin, who echoed Donald Trump's baseless claims of a stolen 2020 election and defended Jan. 6, 2021, U.S. Capitol rioters, was nominated by the president on Monday to be the top federal prosecutor in Washington on a permanent basis.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How President Trump is expanding the powers of the presidency
item.description: President Trump is pushing the limits of presidential power, and the courts are pushing back. MPR News host Angela Davis talks about what a president can and cannot do under the law.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Maja Beckstrom, MPR News

item.title: Washington Post backs out of 'Fire Elon Musk' ad order
item.description: The Washington Post this week backed out of a “Fire Elon Musk” advertising order that was to run as a wrap on some of its Tuesday editions, according to the advocacy group Common Cause.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: White House limits AP access to Oval Office, Air Force One indefinitely
item.description: The White House says it will limit Associated Press journalists' access to the Oval Office and Air Force One, an escalation of a brooding conflict between the Trump administration and the wire service this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5
